applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile(mode='default')
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile with mode='default' to the ModelNew class, which fuses Conv2d + BatchNorm2d + scaling operations into optimized kernels. Achieved 1.74x speedup (0.97ms vs 1.69ms baseline).

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton elementwise scaling kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton kernel overhead was higher than native PyTorch multiplication (1.82ms vs 1.71ms)

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton fused BN+scaling kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Triton kernel overhead too high (2.68ms vs 1.70ms)

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile without explicit mode
attempted_but_failed.reason: Hit Triton compilation errors with async_copy_global_to_local

notes: torch.compile(mode='default') is highly effective for this Conv2d+BN+Scaling pattern on AMD MI355X
Manual Triton kernels had higher overhead than PyTorch's optimized operations for this workload
The problem size (128x64x126x126 output tensor) is well-suited for torch.compile fusion
